metadata.base_standard_adopted: NFPA 13 via 2022 California Building Code Chapter 35
metadata.layer_description: Local (LA-specific) — overrides both NFPA 13 base and California state amendments when in conflict
metadata.note: Always verify against current LAMC as amendments may be updated. Delays of up to 3 months between legislation and posting at amlegal.com.

amendments.description: LA adopts Chapter 35 of the 2022 California Building Code (which itself adopts and amends NFPA 13). Sprinkler provisions in CBC Chapters 4 and 9 also apply.
amendments.ai_impact: Layer 3 starts here. All LAMC rules below sit on top of CBC Chapter 35 which sits on top of NFPA 13.

amendments.description: LA adds the definition of 'Water Curtain' which does not exist in base NFPA 13.

requirement.definition: A line of closely spaced fire sprinklers (or a single sprinkler) aligned adjacent to openings to keep fire from penetrating those openings.

amendments.ai_impact: When AI detects an opening requiring fire separation on the floor plan, it must apply water curtain rules (see LA-003).

amendments.description: Water curtains shall consist of closely spaced sprinklers and noncombustible/limited-combustible draft stops. Specific spacing and placement rules apply.

condition.applies_to: openings requiring fire separation (water curtain installations)

requirement.cross_baffles_required_when: sprinklers are closer than 6 ft (1828.8 mm) apart

amendments.ai_impact: When placing sprinklers at a fire-separation opening, max 6 ft apart, 6–12 inches from draft stop, on the side away from the opening.

amendments.description: Floor control valves must be in specific locations. LA adds stricter placement requirements not in base NFPA 13.

required_locations: within the vestibule of a smokeproof enclosure
on the access balcony of a smokeproof enclosure

exception.condition: buildings with 3 or fewer stories OR where there is no stairway that serves the floor

amendments.ai_impact: Floor plan validator should flag floor control valve placement. For AI sprinkler head placement (Phase 5–7), this is less directly relevant — but important for Phase 8 validator.

amendments.description: When fire sprinkler systems are required in buildings of undetermined use, LA mandates a minimum hazard classification and design area more conservative than the NFPA 13 base standard.

condition.applies_to: buildings where use/occupancy is not specified at time of permit

amendments.ai_impact: If floor plan room color = orange (OH2) AND use is undetermined, apply 3,000 sq ft minimum design area for hydraulic calculations. This is the LA default for most shell buildings.

amendments.description: Sprinkler provisions in CBC Chapter 9 apply in addition to NFPA 13. CBC Chapter 9 governs when sprinkler systems are required in various occupancy types.

requirement.note: CBC Chapter 9 determines when a sprinkler system is required. LAMC 94.2010 governs how it is installed once required.

amendments.ai_impact: Determines which rooms/floors need sprinklers at all. The AI's color-coded input layer (white = needs sprinklers, gray = exempt) encodes this upstream of the AI model.

layer_override_summary.description: When a conflict exists between rules, LAMC wins. Below is the hierarchy for the most common conflict scenarios.

conflicts.topic: Water curtain spacing at fire-separation openings
conflicts.nfpa_13_says: No specific water curtain definition or rule
conflicts.lamc_says: Max 6 ft apart, 6–12 in from draft stop
Must be in stair enclosure or smokeproof vestibule (with 3-story exception)

conflicts.nfpa_13_says: Seismic bracing per Chapter 18 (updated in 2025 edition)
conflicts.california_says: California Seismic Design Category D/E/F, Ip = 1.5 for life-safety systems
conflicts.lamc_says: Follows CBC Chapter 35 which incorporates California seismic amendments
conflicts.winner: California amendment (enforced through LAMC adoption of CBC)
